title: Capella
overview: When Ganta Nakami goes into the observatory, rumored to be haunted, he finds his classmate Isaki Magari sound asleep! They learn they're both struggling with the same problem.

title: Mu Scorpii
overview: Ganta and Isaki have experienced lonely nights as they each struggle with sleeplessness. They form a "Nightly Fun Society" using the observatory as a secret base.

title: Fomalhaut
overview: When another student expresses interest in joining the Astronomy Club, it makes things a lot more complicated for Ganta!

title: Venus
overview: Ganta invites Isaki to the fireworks festival, swearing that they'd only be going together for "photography practice," and Isaki promptly accepts.

title: Canopus
overview: On a cloudy seaside class trip, Isaki makes a promise with Ganta to meet on the beach under the starry sky if the weather clears up.

title: Shooting Star
overview: The heat is unbearable! The lack of AC doesn’t help. Even the breeze is too warm. It's almost too hot to take pictures.

title: The Fireworks Star
overview: Isaki and Ganta try to hand out fliers at the Shokusai Market for a meteor shower viewing party. Will anyone take them up on their offer?

title: The Congregation Star
overview: It's almost time for summer break! If that weren't enough to be excited about, the Astronomy Club plans a meteor shower viewing party.

title: Altair and Vega
overview: After the viewing party was rained out, Ganta plans to shoot pictures of the starry night sky and then use them to enter a contest.

title: Spica of Virgo
overview: Despite starting a part-time job, Ganta hasn’t raised enough money for training camp. But thanks to Isaki staying at her grandmother's house, their problems are solved.

title: The First Stars of Dawn
overview: On the second day of their training camp, Ganta and Isaki's adventure begins with a trip to Mitsukejima.

title: Planet
overview: With their chaperone Haya now gone, Ganta and Isaki are left all alone at her grandmother's house.

title: Methuselah
overview: Isaki's parents find out that she and Ganta have been staying all by themselves. Naturally, they demand that Isaki come home right away, but she refuses.
